payload.instructions: You are running inside the noaide proxy. Provider transcripts surface in the operator console.

payload.message: The healthcheck on /api/ready intermittently fails under load. Can you reproduce it locally and propose a fix?

payload.text: The user wants to debug an intermittent /api/ready failure. I will read the readiness handler, then run the load test that reproduces the issue.

content.text: I will start by reading the readiness handler and the integration test that exercises /api/ready under load.

payload.message: Reading server/src/health.rs and tests/load_ready.rs to understand the path the failing requests take.

payload.text: The handler holds a read lock for the duration of the database probe. Under contention this blocks new requests. I should change it to a lock-free check or use a try_read with a short timeout.

content.text:
The readiness handler holds an RwLock read guard while it pings the database. When the load test issues 200 concurrent requests, lock contention queues new requests behind the slowest probe and a few of them exceed the 1500 ms timeout.

Proposed fix: replace the long-held read lock with `try_read` plus a 50 ms ceiling, and short-circuit to the cached last-known-good status when contention is detected. I will draft the patch and a regression test.

payload.message: Drafting patch for server/src/health.rs and a regression test that pins the failure mode before the fix.
Patch and test look good. Can you run the load test against the patched binary and report tail latency?

payload.text: I will rebuild with the patch applied, then issue 200 concurrent requests against /api/ready and report p50, p99, and the failure count before and after.

content.text: Running the load test now. I will report p50, p99, and failure counts as soon as the run finishes.

payload.message: Build complete. Starting load test: 200 concurrent clients, 60 seconds, target /api/ready.
Load test finished. Before patch: p50 12 ms, p99 1480 ms, 17 failures. After patch: p50 9 ms, p99 41 ms, 0 failures.

content.text: Tail latency dropped from 1480 ms to 41 ms at p99 and the 17 timeouts are gone. The regression test now fails on the unpatched binary and passes on the patched one, so future regressions of this lock pattern will be caught in CI.

payload.message: Great. While you have context, can you sketch a follow-up to refactor the cache layer? Just bullet points.

content.text:
Cache refactor follow-up:
- Move from RwLock<HashMap> to evmap or arc-swap for snapshot reads.
- Promote per-key eviction over the current global TTL sweep.
- Add cache-hit ratio + p99 read latency to the metrics endpoint.
- Wire the readiness probe to the new cache so a stale snapshot still answers in under 5 ms.

payload.message: Outline saved as a thread comment for the refactor planning session.

**Session summary**
- Diagnosed readiness handler lock contention.
- Patched server/src/health.rs to use try_read with 50 ms ceiling and last-known-good fallback.
- Added regression test pinning the failure mode.
- Verified p99 latency dropped from 1480 ms to 41 ms; 17 failures became 0.
- Drafted cache refactor follow-up bullets.

payload.instructions: Continued after context compaction; full transcript is preserved in the noaide audit log.

payload.message: Open a tracking issue for the cache refactor with those bullets as the body.

payload.text: I will draft the issue body, mirror the bullets verbatim, and tag it with the refactor and performance labels.

content.text: Drafted issue: Title `refactor(cache): snapshot reads + per-key eviction + readiness wiring`, body uses the four bullets verbatim, labels `area:cache`, `type:refactor`, `perf`.

payload.message: Issue ready for the operator to review and post.
